evals.name: happy-path-quality-framework
evals.prompt: Design a data quality framework for our customer analytics platform
evals.expected_output: Complete framework with profiling baseline, validation rules, data contracts, anomaly detection, remediation workflows, and SLA monitoring
expectations: Establishes profiling baseline for critical datasets
Defines validation rules with severity classification
Specifies data contracts between producer and consumer teams
Includes anomaly detection with threshold calibration
Designs remediation workflows with quarantine pattern

evals.name: edge-case-no-historical-baseline
evals.prompt: Set up data quality monitoring but we have no historical data to baseline against
evals.expected_output: Uses first 30 days as baseline with wider thresholds (4-sigma), tightening gradually
expectations: Accepts higher initial false positive rate

evals.name: happy-path-data-contracts
evals.prompt: Set up data contracts between our payments team and the analytics team
evals.expected_output: Data contract specification with schema, freshness SLA, volume bounds, quality thresholds, and enforcement mechanisms
expectations: Specifies enforcement via Great Expectations or Soda

evals.name: false-positive-dbt-model-test
evals.prompt: Write dbt tests for my revenue model schema
evals.expected_output: This skill should NOT be triggered; dbt model testing uses analytics-engineering skill
expectations: Skill is not invoked for dbt-specific test writing

evals.name: edge-case-third-party-data
evals.prompt: How do we ensure quality for data we receive from external vendors?
evals.expected_output: Monitoring-first approach with quarantine, aspirational contracts, and 2-3x more manual remediation budget
expectations: Acknowledges no control over producer quality
Emphasizes monitoring and quarantine as essential
Treats contracts as aspirational for external sources
